<!-- @generated by usage-cli from usage spec -->
# `nise mcp`

- **Usage**: `nise mcp`
- **Source code**: [`src/cli/mcp.rs`](https://github.com/jdx/mise/blob/main/src/cli/mcp.rs)

Run Model Context Protocol (MCP) server

This command starts an MCP server that exposes nise functionality
to AI assistants over stdin/stdout using JSON-RPC protocol.

The MCP server provides access to:
- Installed and available tools
- Task definitions and execution
- Environment variables
- Configuration information
- Task execution via the run_task tool

Resources available:
- nise://tools - List all tools (use ?include_inactive=true to include inactive tools)
- nise://tasks - List all tasks with their configurations
- nise://env - List all environment variables
- nise://config - Show configuration files and project root

Tools available:
- install_tool - Install a tool with an optional version (not yet implemented)
- run_task - Execute a nise task with optional arguments

Note: This is primarily intended for integration with AI assistants like Claude,
Cursor, or other tools that support the Model Context Protocol.

Examples:

```
# Start the MCP server (typically used by AI assistant tools)
$ nise mcp

# Example integration with Claude Desktop (add to claude_desktop_config.json):
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "nise": {
      "command": "nise",
      "args": ["mcp"],
      "env": {}
    }
  }
}

# Interactive testing with JSON-RPC commands:
$ echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}}}' | nise mcp

# Resources you can query:
- nise://tools - List active tools
- nise://tools?include_inactive=true - List all installed tools
- nise://tasks - List all tasks
- nise://env - List environment variables
- nise://config - Show configuration info

# Tools available:
- install_tool - Install a tool (not yet implemented)
- run_task - Execute a nise task with optional arguments
  Example: {"task": "build", "args": ["--verbose"]}
```
